title: Evaluate accuracy
text: can run evaluation parameters nowcast_cl().nowcast_eval() one additional parameter: n_past, controls many steps past wish run nowcast . return S7 object 2 slots 2 datasets. nc_eval_obj@detail contains detailed results, nc_eval_obj@summary summarised group_cols delay.
code: library(nowcastr) nc_eval_obj <-   nowcast_demo %>%   nowcast_eval(     n_past = 10,     max_delay = 5,     max_reportunits = 8,     do_model_fitting = FALSE,     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group",     time_units = "weeks"   ) nc_eval_obj@detail #> # A tibble: 238 × 12 #>    group cut_date   date_occurrence last_r_date value value_predicted value_true #>    <chr> <date>     <date>          <date>      <dbl>           <dbl>      <dbl> #>  1 SARS… 2025-09-08 2025-08-04      2025-09-08     47            87.4        143 #>  2 SARS… 2025-09-08 2025-08-11      2025-09-08     19            50.1        112 #>  3 SARS… 2025-09-15 2025-08-11      2025-09-15     18            31.7        112 #>  4 SARS… 2025-09-08 2025-08-18      2025-09-08     10            46.5        123 #>  5 SARS… 2025-09-15 2025-08-18      2025-09-15     11            26.4        123 #>  6 SARS… 2025-09-22 2025-08-18      2025-09-22     40            84.2        123 #>  7 SARS… 2025-09-08 2025-08-25      2025-09-08      5            58.6        107 #>  8 SARS… 2025-09-15 2025-08-25      2025-09-15      6            23.4        107 #>  9 SARS… 2025-09-22 2025-08-25      2025-09-22     30            98.2        107 #> 10 SARS… 2025-09-29 2025-08-25      2025-09-29     40            98.5        107 #> # ℹ 228 more rows #> # ℹ 5 more variables: delay <dbl>, SAPE_pred <dbl>, SAPE_obs <dbl>, #> #   SAPE_improvement <dbl>, isWin <int> nc_eval_obj@summary #> # A tibble: 24 × 10 #>    group        delay n_periods n_obs smape_diff_med smape_diff_q1 smape_diff_q3 #>    <chr>        <dbl>     <int> <int>          <dbl>         <dbl>         <dbl> #>  1 SARS-CoV-2 …     0        10    10        0             0            0        #>  2 SARS-CoV-2 …     1        10    10        0             0            0.375    #>  3 SARS-CoV-2 …     2        10    10        0.619         0.417        0.671    #>  4 SARS-CoV-2 …     3        10    10        0.507         0.289        0.636    #>  5 SARS-CoV-2 …     4        10    10        0.410         0.222        0.493    #>  6 SARS-CoV-2 …     5        10    10        0.368         0.190        0.422    #>  7 SARS-CoV-2 …     0         8     8        0.431         0.420        0.436    #>  8 SARS-CoV-2 …     1        10    10       -0.0782       -0.100       -0.0399   #>  9 SARS-CoV-2 …     2        10    10       -0.00756      -0.0177       0.00859  #> 10 SARS-CoV-2 …     3        10    10       -0.00603      -0.00745      0.000239 #> # ℹ 14 more rows #> # ℹ 3 more variables: winrate <dbl>, winrate_low <dbl>, winrate_high <dbl>

title: Evaluate accuracy
text: predicted values observed values (.e. reported time) last reported values (ground truth)
code: plot_nowcast_eval(nc_eval_obj, delay = 0) # library(ggplot2) plot_nowcast_eval_by_delay(nc_eval_obj, indicator = "smape_diff_med") +   ggplot2::facet_wrap(. ~ group, scales = "free_y") plot_nowcast_eval_detail(nc_eval_obj, delay = 0)

title: Evaluate accuracy
text: can test accuracy nowcasts improve without fill_future_reported_values():
code: nc_eval_obj_with_fill <-   nowcast_demo %>%   fill_future_reported_values(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group",     max_delay = "auto"   ) %>%   nowcast_eval(     n_past = 10,     max_delay = 5,     max_reportunits = 8,     do_model_fitting = FALSE,     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group",     time_units = "weeks"   ) library(dplyr) indicator <- "smape_diff_med"  scenario_a <- nc_eval_obj@summary %>%   dplyr::select("group", "delay", "smape_diff_med") %>%   dplyr::mutate(scenario = "No fill")  scenario_b <- nc_eval_obj_with_fill@summary %>%   dplyr::select("group", "delay", "smape_diff_med") %>%   dplyr::mutate(scenario = "Fill")  ## quick mean of everything dplyr::bind_rows(scenario_a, scenario_b) %>%   dplyr::filter(delay <= 3) %>% ## predictions for older data are not that interesting   dplyr::summarise(     .by = c(scenario),     avg_smape_diff_med = mean(smape_diff_med, na.rm = T)   ) %>%   dplyr::mutate(tag = dplyr::if_else(avg_smape_diff_med == max(avg_smape_diff_med), "best overall", "")) %>%   print() #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   scenario avg_smape_diff_med tag            #>   <chr>                 <dbl> <chr>          #> 1 No fill              0.116  "best overall" #> 2 Fill                 0.0835 "" ## Comparison plot library(ggplot2) dplyr::bind_rows(scenario_a, scenario_b) %>%   dplyr::summarise(     .by = c(delay, scenario, group),     smape_diff_med = mean(smape_diff_med, na.rm = T)   ) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = delay, y = smape_diff_med, color = scenario)) +   geom_point() +   geom_line() +   facet_wrap(~group, scales = "free_y") +   # ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent()) +   theme_nowcastr() +   labs(     y = "Differential sMAPE",     x = "Delay",     color = "Scenario",     title = "Compare Differential sMAPE of 2 scenarios",     subtitle = "Higher is better"   ) # devtools::load_all("~/gh/nowcastr")  library(dplyr) {   results <- tibble()   results_details <- tibble()   for (ii in seq(2, 15, 1)) {     nc_eval_obj_i <-       nowcast_demo %>%       filter(group == "Syndromic ILI") %>%       nowcast_eval(         n_past = 99,         max_delay = 3,         max_reportunits = ii, ## this varies         do_model_fitting = FALSE,         col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,         col_date_reporting = date_report,         col_value = value,         group_cols = "group",         time_units = "weeks"       )     res_i <- nc_eval_obj_i@summary %>% mutate(max_reportunits = ii)     res_d_i <- nc_eval_obj_i@detail %>% mutate(max_reportunits = ii)      results <- bind_rows(results, res_i)     results_details <- bind_rows(results_details, res_d_i)      # avg <- mean(res_i$smape_diff_med) |> signif(2)     # message(max_reportunits, ": ", avg)   } }  library(ggplot2) results %>%   filter(group == "Syndromic ILI") %>%   filter(delay < 3) %>%   summarise(     .by = c(group, max_reportunits, delay),     avg_smape_diff_med = mean(smape_diff_med, na.rm = T)   ) %>%   mutate(     .by = c(group, delay),     best = if_else(avg_smape_diff_med == max(avg_smape_diff_med), "best", "")   ) %>%   mutate(delay = factor(delay)) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = max_reportunits, y = avg_smape_diff_med, color = delay, group = delay)) +   geom_line() +   geom_point() +   theme_nowcastr() +   # geom_text(aes(label = best)) +   # geom_point(color='red') +   # geom_point(aes(fill = best == 'best')) +   geom_point(shape = 21, aes(fill = best == "best")) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "transparent", "TRUE" = "red"), guide = "none") +   scale_color_viridis_d(option = "mako", begin = .4, end = .8) +   # ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent()) +   # facet_wrap(. ~ delay, scales = "free_y") +   facet_wrap(. ~ group, scales = "free_y") +   labs(y = "Differential sMAPE", x = "max_reportunits", color = "Delay") results %>%   filter(group == "Syndromic ILI") %>%   filter(delay < 3) %>%   summarise(     .by = c(max_reportunits),     n = n(),     avg_smape_diff_med = mean(smape_diff_med, na.rm = T)   ) %>%   mutate(tag = if_else(avg_smape_diff_med == max(avg_smape_diff_med), "best overall", "")) %>%   print() #> # A tibble: 14 × 4 #>    max_reportunits     n avg_smape_diff_med tag            #>              <dbl> <int>              <dbl> <chr>          #>  1               2     3             0.0865 ""             #>  2               3     3             0.112  ""             #>  3               4     3             0.127  ""             #>  4               5     3             0.141  ""             #>  5               6     3             0.140  ""             #>  6               7     3             0.146  "best overall" #>  7               8     3             0.142  ""             #>  8               9     3             0.137  ""             #>  9              10     3             0.138  ""             #> 10              11     3             0.140  ""             #> 11              12     3             0.140  ""             #> 12              13     3             0.140  ""             #> 13              14     3             0.142  ""             #> 14              15     3             0.143  ""

title: Evaluate accuracy
code: # devtools::load_all("~/gh/nowcastr")  library(dplyr) {   results <- tibble()   results_details <- tibble()   for (ii in seq(2, 15, 1)) {     nc_eval_obj_i <-       nowcast_demo %>%       filter(group == "Syndromic ILI") %>%       nowcast_eval(         n_past = 99,         max_delay = 3,         max_reportunits = ii, ## this varies         do_model_fitting = FALSE,         col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,         col_date_reporting = date_report,         col_value = value,         group_cols = "group",         time_units = "weeks"       )     res_i <- nc_eval_obj_i@summary %>% mutate(max_reportunits = ii)     res_d_i <- nc_eval_obj_i@detail %>% mutate(max_reportunits = ii)      results <- bind_rows(results, res_i)     results_details <- bind_rows(results_details, res_d_i)      # avg <- mean(res_i$smape_diff_med) |> signif(2)     # message(max_reportunits, ": ", avg)   } }  library(ggplot2) results %>%   filter(group == "Syndromic ILI") %>%   filter(delay < 3) %>%   summarise(     .by = c(group, max_reportunits, delay),     avg_smape_diff_med = mean(smape_diff_med, na.rm = T)   ) %>%   mutate(     .by = c(group, delay),     best = if_else(avg_smape_diff_med == max(avg_smape_diff_med), "best", "")   ) %>%   mutate(delay = factor(delay)) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = max_reportunits, y = avg_smape_diff_med, color = delay, group = delay)) +   geom_line() +   geom_point() +   theme_nowcastr() +   # geom_text(aes(label = best)) +   # geom_point(color='red') +   # geom_point(aes(fill = best == 'best')) +   geom_point(shape = 21, aes(fill = best == "best")) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "transparent", "TRUE" = "red"), guide = "none") +   scale_color_viridis_d(option = "mako", begin = .4, end = .8) +   # ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent()) +   # facet_wrap(. ~ delay, scales = "free_y") +   facet_wrap(. ~ group, scales = "free_y") +   labs(y = "Differential sMAPE", x = "max_reportunits", color = "Delay") results %>%   filter(group == "Syndromic ILI") %>%   filter(delay < 3) %>%   summarise(     .by = c(max_reportunits),     n = n(),     avg_smape_diff_med = mean(smape_diff_med, na.rm = T)   ) %>%   mutate(tag = if_else(avg_smape_diff_med == max(avg_smape_diff_med), "best overall", "")) %>%   print() #> # A tibble: 14 × 4 #>    max_reportunits     n avg_smape_diff_med tag            #>              <dbl> <int>              <dbl> <chr>          #>  1               2     3             0.0865 ""             #>  2               3     3             0.112  ""             #>  3               4     3             0.127  ""             #>  4               5     3             0.141  ""             #>  5               6     3             0.140  ""             #>  6               7     3             0.146  "best overall" #>  7               8     3             0.142  ""             #>  8               9     3             0.137  ""             #>  9              10     3             0.138  ""             #> 10              11     3             0.140  ""             #> 11              12     3             0.140  ""             #> 12              13     3             0.140  ""             #> 13              14     3             0.142  ""             #> 14              15     3             0.143  ""

title: Getting started
text: package available GitHub. Install :
code: pak::pak("whocov/nowcastr") library(nowcastr)

title: Getting started
text: dataset must contain least three columns: occurrence date: event happened reporting date: event reported value: observed count/value <groups>: none, one multiple grouping columns: e.g. group_cols = "species" # group_cols = c("region", "disease") package includes demo dataset nowcast_demo follows structure demo data also includes group column demonstrating grouped processing, though can multiple grouping columns.

title: Getting started
text: typical nowcasting workflow nowcastr involves following steps. nowcasting, inspect reporting pattern data:  “millipede” plot provides alternative view delays. reporting date mapped distinct line color.  Depending data use case, may want fill missing values last known reported value.  step optional; nowcast_cl can handle unfilled data. Perform nowcasting using chain-ladder method: nowcast_cl() function returns nowcast_results object containing predictions, delay distributions, completeness estimates, parameters. Access different datasets. Plot results:   Open Shiny app explore results group group:
code: nowcast_demo %>%   plot_nc_input(     option = "triangle",     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group" # use a vector for multiple columns: c("column1", "column2"... )   ) nowcast_demo %>%   plot_nc_input(     option = "millipede",     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group"   ) data_filled <- nowcast_demo %>%   fill_future_reported_values(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group",     max_delay = "auto"   ) data_filled %>%   plot_nc_input(     option = "triangle",     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group"   ) nc_obj <-   data_filled %>%   nowcast_cl(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group",     time_units = "weeks",     # max_delay = 5,     # max_reportunits = 8,     do_model_fitting = TRUE   ) S7::prop_names(nc_obj) #>  [1] "name"         "params"       "time_start"   "time_end"     "n_groups"     #>  [6] "max_delay"    "data"         "completeness" "delays"       "models"       #> [11] "results" nc_obj@results # Final nowcasted values #> # A tibble: 95 × 7 #>    group    date_occurrence last_r_date delay value value_predicted completeness #>    <chr>    <date>          <date>      <dbl> <dbl>           <dbl>        <dbl> #>  1 SARS-Co… 2025-11-17      2025-11-17      0     0             0        0.00391 #>  2 SARS-Co… 2025-11-10      2025-11-17      1     0             0        0.0316  #>  3 SARS-Co… 2025-11-03      2025-11-17      2     1            11.4      0.0878  #>  4 SARS-Co… 2025-10-27      2025-11-17      3     6            36.5      0.164   #>  5 SARS-Co… 2025-10-20      2025-11-17      4     7            27.8      0.252   #>  6 SARS-Co… 2025-10-13      2025-11-17      5    21            61.5      0.341   #>  7 SARS-Co… 2025-10-06      2025-11-17      6    52           122.       0.427   #>  8 SARS-Co… 2025-09-29      2025-11-17      7    55           109.       0.507   #>  9 SARS-Co… 2025-09-22      2025-11-17      8    70           121.       0.577   #> 10 SARS-Co… 2025-09-15      2025-11-17      9    60            93.9      0.639   #> # ℹ 85 more rows nc_obj@delays # Summarised completeness values by delay #> # A tibble: 96 × 5 #>    group                      delay     n completeness_obs completeness_modelled #>    <chr>                      <dbl> <int>            <dbl>                 <dbl> #>  1 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admis…     0    10          0.0169                0.00391 #>  2 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admis…     1    10          0.00670               0.0316  #>  3 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admis…     2    10          0.0646                0.0878  #>  4 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admis…     3    10          0.163                 0.164   #>  5 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admis…     4    10          0.250                 0.252   #>  6 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admis…     5    10          0.321                 0.341   #>  7 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admis…     6    10          0.442                 0.427   #>  8 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admis…     7    10          0.537                 0.507   #>  9 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admis…     8    10          0.611                 0.577   #> 10 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admis…     9    10          0.668                 0.639   #> # ℹ 86 more rows nc_obj@completeness # Detailed completeness estimates #> # A tibble: 2,478 × 8 #>    group         date_occurrence date_report value delay last_value completeness #>    <chr>         <date>          <date>      <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>        <dbl> #>  1 SARS-CoV-2 H… 2025-11-17      2025-11-17      0     0          0        1     #>  2 SARS-CoV-2 H… 2025-11-10      2025-11-17      0     1          0        1     #>  3 SARS-CoV-2 H… 2025-11-10      2025-11-10      0     0          0        1     #>  4 SARS-CoV-2 H… 2025-11-03      2025-11-17      1     2          1        1     #>  5 SARS-CoV-2 H… 2025-11-03      2025-11-10      0     1          1        0     #>  6 SARS-CoV-2 H… 2025-11-03      2025-11-03      0     0          1        0     #>  7 SARS-CoV-2 H… 2025-10-27      2025-11-17      6     3          6        1     #>  8 SARS-CoV-2 H… 2025-10-27      2025-11-10      2     2          6        0.333 #>  9 SARS-CoV-2 H… 2025-10-27      2025-11-03      0     1          6        0     #> 10 SARS-CoV-2 H… 2025-10-20      2025-11-17      7     4          7        1     #> # ℹ 2,468 more rows #> # ℹ 1 more variable: reportweight <dbl> # Delay distribution plot(nc_obj, which = "delays") +   ggplot2::labs(     caption = NULL,     subtitle = paste0("From data reported on: ", max(data_filled$date_report))   ) # Nowcast time series plot(nc_obj, which = "results") +   ggplot2::labs(     caption = NULL,     subtitle = paste0("From data reported on: ", max(data_filled$date_report))   ) nowcast_explore(nc_obj)

title: Getting started
text: nowcasting, inspect reporting pattern data:  “millipede” plot provides alternative view delays. reporting date mapped distinct line color.
code: nowcast_demo %>%   plot_nc_input(     option = "triangle",     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group" # use a vector for multiple columns: c("column1", "column2"... )   ) nowcast_demo %>%   plot_nc_input(     option = "millipede",     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group"   )

title: Getting started
text: Depending data use case, may want fill missing values last known reported value.  step optional; nowcast_cl can handle unfilled data.
code: data_filled <- nowcast_demo %>%   fill_future_reported_values(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group",     max_delay = "auto"   ) data_filled %>%   plot_nc_input(     option = "triangle",     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group"   )

title: Getting started
text: Perform nowcasting using chain-ladder method: nowcast_cl() function returns nowcast_results object containing predictions, delay distributions, completeness estimates, parameters.
code: nc_obj <-   data_filled %>%   nowcast_cl(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group",     time_units = "weeks",     # max_delay = 5,     # max_reportunits = 8,     do_model_fitting = TRUE   ) S7::prop_names(nc_obj) #>  [1] "name"         "params"       "time_start"   "time_end"     "n_groups"     #>  [6] "max_delay"    "data"         "completeness" "delays"       "models"       #> [11] "results"

title: Getting started
text: Access different datasets. Plot results:   Open Shiny app explore results group group:

Nowcast time series plot(nc_obj, which = "results") +   ggplot2::labs(     caption = NULL,     subtitle = paste0("From data reported on: ", max(data_filled$date_report))   ) nowcast_explore(nc_obj)

title: Getting started
text: chain-ladder method estimates “completeness” delay bucket: Delay = reporting date - occurrence date Completeness = observed value / last reported value (approximation true value) Average completeness per delay bucket (across occurrence dates) Nowcast = observed value / average completeness Recent occurrence dates shorter delays lower completeness. method upweights observations estimate true count.

title: Getting started
text: retro-score ratio actual value changes maximum possible changes [0-1].  somewhat opposite fill_future_reported_values().
code: # Calculate retro-scores retroscores <- nowcast_demo %>%   calculate_retro_score(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group"   ) print(retroscores) #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   group                          n_changes max_retro_adj retro_score #>   <chr>                              <dbl>         <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1 SARS-CoV-2 non-STL Positivity        385           575       0.670 #> 2 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions       374           575       0.650 #> 3 Syndromic ILI                        371           575       0.645 #> 4 Syndromic ARI                        299           575       0.52 retroscores %>%   ggplot2::ggplot(ggplot2::aes(y = stats::reorder(group, retro_score), x = retro_score)) +   ggplot2::geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "dodgerblue1") +   theme_nowcastr() +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(     limits = c(0, 1),     # labels = scales::label_percent()   ) +   ggplot2::labs(     y = "Group",     x = "Retro-Score"   ) # Remove duplicate reported values (same value and higher reporting date) nowcast_demo %>%   rm_repeated_values(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group"   ) #> # A tibble: 1,624 × 4 #>    value date_occurrence date_report group                          #>    <dbl> <date>          <date>      <chr>                          #>  1    12 2024-12-16      2025-05-26  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  2    31 2024-12-23      2025-05-26  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  3    22 2024-12-30      2025-05-26  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  4    21 2024-12-30      2025-06-02  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  5    18 2025-01-06      2025-05-26  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  6    19 2025-01-06      2025-06-16  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  7    11 2025-01-13      2025-05-26  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  8     7 2025-01-20      2025-05-26  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #>  9     8 2025-01-20      2025-06-16  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #> 10    17 2025-01-27      2025-05-26  SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions #> # ℹ 1,614 more rows

code: # Calculate retro-scores retroscores <- nowcast_demo %>%   calculate_retro_score(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group"   ) print(retroscores) #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   group                          n_changes max_retro_adj retro_score #>   <chr>                              <dbl>         <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1 SARS-CoV-2 non-STL Positivity        385           575       0.670 #> 2 SARS-CoV-2 Hospital Admissions       374           575       0.650 #> 3 Syndromic ILI                        371           575       0.645 #> 4 Syndromic ARI                        299           575       0.52 retroscores %>%   ggplot2::ggplot(ggplot2::aes(y = stats::reorder(group, retro_score), x = retro_score)) +   ggplot2::geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "dodgerblue1") +   theme_nowcastr() +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(     limits = c(0, 1),     # labels = scales::label_percent()   ) +   ggplot2::labs(     y = "Group",     x = "Retro-Score"   )

title: Authors and Citation
text: Leroy M, Campbell F (2026). nowcastr: Nowcasting Chain-Ladder Method. R package version 0.2.1.9000, https://github.com/whocov/nowcastr.
code: @Manual{,   title = {nowcastr: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method},   author = {Mathias Leroy and Finlay Campbell},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.1.9000},   url = {https://github.com/whocov/nowcastr}, }

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method
text: R package nowcasting non-cumulative chain-ladder method. nowcast_cl() returns object intermediary results plot_nc_input(option = "triangle") / plot(= "data", option = "triangle") plot_nc_input(option = "millipede") / plot(= "data", option = "millipede") plot_delays() / plot(= "delays") plot_nowcast() / plot(= "results") calculate_retro_score(): Calculate retro-scores groups rm_repeated_values(): Remove duplicated reported values reporting matrix fill_future_reported_values(): Fill future reported values last known values nowcast_eval(): perform evaluation plot_nowcast_eval(): plot main eval results plot_nowcast_eval_by_delay(): plot eval results delay plot_nowcast_eval_detail(): plot detailed eval results

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method
code: ## install from GitHub pak::pak("whocov/nowcastr") # recommended, more up to date versions  ## install from CRAN install.packages("nowcastr")

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method
text: detailed examples available Getting started vignette.
code: library(nowcastr)  ## Get your data nc_data <- nowcast_demo  ## Plot input data nc_data %>%   plot_nc_input(     option = "triangle", # or "millipede"     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group"   )  ## Run nowcast with built-in demo data nc_obj <- nc_data %>%    nowcast_cl(     max_delay = 5, # optional     max_reportunits = 8, # optional     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = "group",     time_units = "weeks",     do_model_fitting = TRUE   )  ## Plot nowcasted time series plot(nc_obj, which = "results") print(nc_obj@results) # inspect data frame  ## Plot delay distribution plot(nc_obj, which = "delays") print(nc_obj@delays) # inspect data frame

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method
text: Dataset least 2 date columns value column. dataset can also multiple group-columns batch processing. Note delays (difference 2 dates) constant intervals, .e., multiples 1 day 7 days.
code: dplyr::glimpse(nowcast_demo, 70)  # Rows: 1,624 # Columns: 4 # $ value           <dbl> 251563, 219818, 219815, 253451, 253454, 3116… # $ date_occurrence <date> 2024-12-16, 2024-12-23, 2024-12-23, 2024-12… # $ date_report     <date> 2025-05-26, 2025-05-26, 2025-06-02, 2025-05… # $ group           <chr> "Syndromic ARI", "Syndromic ARI", "Syndromic…

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method
text: nowcast_cl() returns S7 object class nowcast_results following slots (access @):

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method
text: Input Data: Ensure three core columns: observed_value / date_of_reporting / date_of_occurrence (e.g. date_of_event / date_of_onset) Calculate reporting_delay (= date_of_reporting - date_of_occurrence) Compute completeness (= observed_value / true_value (approximated last_reported_value)) Aggregate avg_completeness reporting_delay Optional: Fit curve Apply Nowcast: nowcast = observed_value / avg_completeness

what: Calculate retro-scores for all groups — calculate_retro_score
title: Calculate retro-scores for all groups — calculate_retro_score
text: retro-score amount retro-adjustments / max possible retro-adjustments higher better nowcast_cl() retro_score = n_changes / max_changes = retro_adjustments / max_retro_adj Notes: "retro-adjustments" = "value changes" retro-score = number changes / number ywks (max changes)

title: Calculate retro-scores for all groups — calculate_retro_score
code: calculate_retro_score(   df,   col_date_occurrence,   col_date_reporting,   col_value,   group_cols = NULL,   method = "2D_allgroups",   max_delay = Inf,   aggrby )

title: Calculate retro-scores for all groups — calculate_retro_score
text: df data.frame tibble. col_date_occurrence Column name date occurrence/reference. col_date_reporting Column name date reporting. col_value Column name value. group_cols Optional character vector column names grouping. method '2D_allgroups' (number changes 2D triangle) 'at_least_1_change_by_occ' (number occurrence dates least 2 reported values) max_delay Maximum delay consider. (works method '2D_allgroups') aggrby character vector column names aggregate .

title: Calculate retro-scores for all groups — calculate_retro_score
text: tibble group cols + retro_score (percentage 0-1)

title: Calculate retro-scores for all groups — calculate_retro_score
code: generate_test_data() %>%   calculate_retro_score(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = NULL     # , aggrby = country     # , method = "at_least_1_change_by_occ"   ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   n_changes max_retro_adj retro_score #>       <dbl>         <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1        72            72           1

what: Fill future reported values with last known values — fill_future_reported_values
title: Fill future reported values with last known values — fill_future_reported_values
text: function completes data frame include combinations occurrence reporting dates (within group). fills missing values carrying last known reported value backward time future reports past reports given occurrence. useful dealing right-censored reporting data reports updated time.

title: Fill future reported values with last known values — fill_future_reported_values
code: fill_future_reported_values(   df,   col_date_occurrence,   col_date_reporting,   col_value,   group_cols = NULL,   max_delay = Inf )

title: Fill future reported values with last known values — fill_future_reported_values
text: df data.frame tibble. col_date_occurrence Column name date occurrence/reference. col_date_reporting Column name date reporting. col_value Column name value. group_cols Optional character vector column names grouping. max_delay Inf / 'auto' / NULL / integer. 'auto' NULL keep max_delay input.

title: Fill future reported values with last known values — fill_future_reported_values
text: data frame columns df, rows added missing reporting dates NA values col_value filled last available observation occurrence date within group.

title: Fill future reported values with last known values — fill_future_reported_values
code: library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: ‘dplyr’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:stats’: #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union  generate_test_data() %>%   fill_future_reported_values(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     group_cols = NULL   ) #> # A tibble: 126 × 4 #>    date_occurrence date_report delay value #>    <date>          <date>      <int> <dbl> #>  1 2025-02-09      2025-02-09      0  50   #>  2 2025-02-08      2025-02-09      1  79.7 #>  3 2025-02-08      2025-02-08      0  50   #>  4 2025-02-07      2025-02-09      2  91.7 #>  5 2025-02-07      2025-02-08      1  79.7 #>  6 2025-02-07      2025-02-07      0  50   #>  7 2025-02-06      2025-02-09      3  96.6 #>  8 2025-02-06      2025-02-08      2  91.7 #>  9 2025-02-06      2025-02-07      1  79.7 #> 10 2025-02-06      2025-02-06      0  50   #> # ℹ 116 more rows

what: Generate asymptotic test data for nowcating — generate_test_data
title: Generate asymptotic test data for nowcating — generate_test_data
text: Create synthetic long-format test data following asymptotic delay curve constant final value. Formula: value = final_value * (c + 1 * (1 - exp(-b * .data$delay))))

title: Generate asymptotic test data for nowcating — generate_test_data
code: generate_test_data(   n_reportdates = 9,   n_delays = 10,   reportdate_from = "2025-02-01",   delay_from = 0,   time_units = "days",   final_value = 100,   c = 0.5,   b = 0.9,   remove_delay = FALSE )

title: Generate asymptotic test data for nowcating — generate_test_data
text: n_reportdates Integer. Number consecutive report dates generate. n_delays Integer. Number delay values generate. reportdate_from Character Date. Start report date (e.g. "2025-02-01"). delay_from Integer >= 0. Minimum delay value. time_units Time units. Accepted values: ("auto", "secs", "mins", "hours", "days", "weeks"). final_value Numeric. Asymptotic target (formula). c Numeric (0-1). Baseline fraction final_value added curve. b Numeric. Rate constant controlling fast y approaches ; larger b → faster approach. remove_delay Logical. TRUE, remove delay column output.

title: Generate asymptotic test data for nowcating — generate_test_data
text: tibble (long format) columns date_report (Date), date_occurrence (Date), delay (integer, optional) value (numeric).

title: Generate asymptotic test data for nowcating — generate_test_data
text: defaults returns n_reportdates × (n_delays - delay_from + 1) rows (9 × 10 = 90). reportdate_from may provided Date parsable character; lubridate units used stepping.

title: Generate asymptotic test data for nowcating — generate_test_data
Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method — nowcast_cl
text: Performs nowcasting using non-cumulative Chain-Ladder Method. Input dataset 2 date columns; 1 value column; flexible number group columns. Output dataset latest reported data joined completeness ratio final value_predicted. option use model-free completeness ratio calculation use model-fitted completeness.

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method — nowcast_cl
code: nowcast_cl(   df,   col_date_occurrence,   col_date_reporting,   col_value,   group_cols = NULL,   time_units = "days",   max_delay = NULL,   max_reportunits = 10,   max_completeness = 5,   min_completeness_samples = 1,   use_weighted_method = TRUE,   do_propagate_missing_delays = FALSE,   do_model_fitting = TRUE,   model_names = c("monomolecular", "vonbertalanffy", "logistic", "gompertz",     "asymptotic", "linear"),   do_use_modelled_completeness = TRUE,   rss_threshold = 0.01 )

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method — nowcast_cl
text: df data.frame tibble. col_date_occurrence Column name date occurrence/reference. col_date_reporting Column name date reporting. col_value Column name value. group_cols Optional character vector column names grouping. time_units Time unit delay calculation. Accepted values: ("auto", "secs", "mins", "hours", "days", "weeks"). max_delay Max delay keep analysis. Interger NULL. NULL, take max delay data. max_reportunits Max number col_date_reporting use (unit time_units). max_completeness Maximum completeness ratio (e.g., 2 = 200%). min_completeness_samples Min number samples required calculate completeness. Integer 1 max_reportunits. use_weighted_method Use weighted method, linear weight older reported completeness values do_propagate_missing_delays Fill missing completeness lower delay value. do_model_fitting Fit model completeness delay. Models useful indicators like 'time 95% compl.' also soften variability. model_names Character vector names models test best fit. Accepted values: "monomolecular", "vonbertalanffy", "logistic", "gompertz", "asymptotic", "linear" do_use_modelled_completeness Use modelled completeness values nowcasting. Boolean NULL (auto selective).  (unused do_model_fitting=FALSE) rss_threshold Minimum RSS threshold use model-fitted values. used do_use_modelled_completeness NULL. RSS fit higher observed completeness used nowcasting.

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method — nowcast_cl
text: Returns S7 object class nowcast_results. object serves comprehensive container analysis results metadata. Use @results slot access primary prediction data frame. object includes: Predictions: results slot contains latest observed values, calculated completeness ratio, value_predicted. Calculation: Predictions derived using \(value\_predicted = value / completeness\). Metadata: Slots params, time_start, max_delay, model diagnostics (RSS).

title: Nowcasting with Chain-Ladder Method — nowcast_cl
code: input <- generate_test_data() res <- input %>%   nowcast_cl(     col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,     col_date_reporting = date_report,     col_value = value,     time_units = "days"   )  # Access the predicted data: head(res@results) #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>   date_occurrence last_r_date delay value value_predicted completeness #>   <date>          <date>      <dbl> <dbl>           <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 2025-02-09      2025-02-09      0  50              98.2        0.509 #> 2 2025-02-08      2025-02-09      1  79.7            99.4        0.802 #> 3 2025-02-07      2025-02-09      2  91.7            99.7        0.920 #> 4 2025-02-06      2025-02-09      3  96.6            99.9        0.968 #> 5 2025-02-05      2025-02-09      4  98.6            99.9        0.987 #> 6 2025-02-04      2025-02-09      5  99.4           100.0        0.995

code: nowcast_demo

title: Evaluate Nowcasting Performance — nowcast_eval
text: Evaluates historical performance nowcast_cl() repeatedly peeling back recent reporting period comparing predictions last reported values (highest col_date_reporting per occurrence date).

title: Evaluate Nowcasting Performance — nowcast_eval
code: nowcast_eval(   df,   col_date_occurrence,   col_date_reporting,   col_value,   group_cols = NULL,   n_past = 10,   time_units = "days",   max_delay = NULL,   max_reportunits = 10,   max_completeness = 5,   min_completeness_samples = 1,   use_weighted_method = TRUE,   do_propagate_missing_delays = FALSE,   do_model_fitting = TRUE,   model_names = c("monomolecular", "vonbertalanffy", "logistic", "gompertz",     "asymptotic", "linear"),   do_use_modelled_completeness = TRUE,   rss_threshold = 0.01 )

title: Evaluate Nowcasting Performance — nowcast_eval
text: df data.frame tibble. col_date_occurrence Column name date occurrence/reference. col_date_reporting Column name date reporting. col_value Column name value. group_cols Optional character vector column names grouping. n_past Integer. Number past reporting periods evaluate. iteration peels one reporting period (time_units). time_units Time unit delay calculation. Accepted values: ("auto", "secs", "mins", "hours", "days", "weeks"). max_delay Max delay keep analysis. Interger NULL. NULL, take max delay data. max_reportunits Max number col_date_reporting use (unit time_units). max_completeness Maximum completeness ratio (e.g., 2 = 200%). min_completeness_samples Min number samples required calculate completeness. Integer 1 max_reportunits. use_weighted_method Use weighted method, linear weight older reported completeness values do_propagate_missing_delays Fill missing completeness lower delay value. do_model_fitting Fit model completeness delay. Models useful indicators like 'time 95% compl.' also soften variability. model_names Character vector names models test best fit. Accepted values: "monomolecular", "vonbertalanffy", "logistic", "gompertz", "asymptotic", "linear" do_use_modelled_completeness Use modelled completeness values nowcasting. Boolean NULL (auto selective).  (unused do_model_fitting=FALSE) rss_threshold Minimum RSS threshold use model-fitted values. used do_use_modelled_completeness NULL. RSS fit higher observed completeness used nowcasting.

title: Evaluate Nowcasting Performance — nowcast_eval
text: S7 object class nowcast_eval_results.

title: Evaluate Nowcasting Performance — nowcast_eval
code: input <- generate_test_data() eval_res <- nowcast_eval(   df = input,   col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,   col_date_reporting = date_report,   col_value = value,   n_past = 10,   time_units = "days" ) #> Warning: n_past (10) exceeds available reporting periods (8). Will be using the max available instead: 7

what: S7 object class for Nowcast Evaluation Results — nowcast_eval_results
title: S7 object class for Nowcast Evaluation Results — nowcast_eval_results
text: object returned nowcast_eval. S7 class following slots (accessible @):

title: S7 object class for Nowcast Evaluation Results — nowcast_eval_results
code: nowcast_eval_results(detail, summary, params, n_past, time_start, time_end)

title: S7 object class for Nowcast Evaluation Results — nowcast_eval_results
text: detail data.frame. summary data.frame. params list. n_past integer. time_start POSIXct. time_end POSIXct.

title: S7 object class for Nowcast Evaluation Results — nowcast_eval_results
text: S7 object class nowcast_eval_results.

title: S7 object class for Nowcast Evaluation Results — nowcast_eval_results
text: detail data.frame per-prediction errors (observed, predicted, last reported values). summary data.frame aggregated SMAPE winrate, group delay. params list parameters used. n_past number past periods evaluated. time_start POSIXct start time. time_end POSIXct end time.

title: S7 object class for Nowcast Evaluation Results — nowcast_eval_results

what: Explore Nowcast Results with Shiny — nowcast_explore
title: Explore Nowcast Results with Shiny — nowcast_explore
text: Launch interactive Shiny application explore visualize nowcast results. app displays summary table model statistics allows users select groups view corresponding plots input data, delay distributions, nowcast results.

title: Explore Nowcast Results with Shiny — nowcast_explore
code: nowcast_explore(nc_obj)

what: S7 object class for nowcast_cl() Results — nowcast_results
title: S7 object class for nowcast_cl() Results — nowcast_results
text: nowcast_cl() function returns object class.

title: S7 object class for nowcast_cl() Results — nowcast_results
code: nowcast_results(name, params, time_start, time_end, n_groups, max_delay,   data, completeness, delays, models, results)

title: S7 object class for nowcast_cl() Results — nowcast_results
text: name character string timestamp run. params list parameters used nowcasting (unevaluated call). time_start sys time function started. time_end sys time function ended. n_groups number groups processed. max_delay maximum delay used. data Dataframe. original input data frame (required columns). completeness Dataframe. original input data frame delays completeness columns. delays Dataframe. data frame final aggregated completeness estimates per delay (+ modelled column do_model_fitting TRUE). models Dataframe. resulting fitted models (empty data frame do_model_fitting FALSE) results Dataframe. data frame resulting nowcasting predictions.

title: S7 object class for nowcast_cl() Results — nowcast_results
text: S7 object class nowcast_results. object structured container entire nowcasting pipeline output. consists following properties (slots): name Character. unique timestamp identifier run (YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS). params List. evaluated parameters arguments used function call. time_start, time_end POSIXct. Timestamps marking duration calculation. n_groups Numeric. total count unique groups processed. max_delay Numeric. maximum reporting delay (time_units) considered. data Data frame. subset original input used analysis. completeness Data frame. Detailed row-level completeness calculations delays. delays Data frame. Aggregated completeness estimates per delay unit, including observed (optionally) modelled values. models Data frame. Results non-linear model fitting, including RSS model types. Returns empty data frame do_model_fitting FALSE. results Data frame. final nowcasting table containing predicted values.

what: Plot Reporting Completeness by Delay — plot_delays
title: Plot Reporting Completeness by Delay — plot_delays
text: Creates scatter plot reporting completeness reporting delay. col_completeness_modelled present, shown dotted line.

title: Plot Reporting Completeness by Delay — plot_delays
code: plot_delays(   df,   col_completeness_obs,   col_completeness_modelled = "",   group_cols = NULL,   color1 = "#222222",   color2 = "firebrick2",   limits_y = c(NA, NA) )

title: Plot Reporting Completeness by Delay — plot_delays
text: df data.frame containing 'delay' col_completeness columns. optional modelled column col_completeness_obs Column name Observed Completeness. (dots) col_completeness_modelled Column name Modelled Completeness. (line) group_cols Optional character vector column names grouping. color1 Color observed data. (dots) color2 Color modelled data. (line) limits_y vector passed limits ggplot2::scale_y_continuous.

title: Plot Reporting Completeness by Delay — plot_delays
text: ggplot object showing completeness vs. delay.

title: Plot Reporting Completeness by Delay — plot_delays
code: delays <- data.frame(   delay = 0:9,   completeness = c(0.509, 0.802, 0.920, 0.967, 0.987, 0.995, 0.998, 0.999, 1, NA),   modelled = c(0.509, 0.802, 0.920, 0.968, 0.987, 0.995, 0.998, 0.999, 1, 1) ) plot_delays(   df = delays,   col_completeness_obs = completeness,   col_completeness_modelled = modelled ) #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).

title: Plot Nowcast Input Data — plot_nc_input
text: df data.frame tibble. col_value Column name value. col_date_occurrence Column name date occurrence/reference. col_date_reporting Column name date reporting. group_cols Optional character vector column names grouping. option "millipede" "triangle". do_rescale Rescale values 0-1. Boolean. do_facet_groups Boolean. groups faceted?

title: Plot Nowcasting Predictions — plot_nowcast
text: Compares observed data nowcasted predictions occurrence date. Observed values plotted solid grey line, predicted values dashed black line.

title: Plot Nowcasting Predictions — plot_nowcast
code: plot_nowcast(   df,   col_date_occurrence,   col_value,   col_value_predicted,   group_cols = NULL,   color1 = "#333333",   color2 = "firebrick1" )

title: Plot Nowcasting Predictions — plot_nowcast
text: df data.frame tibble. col_date_occurrence Column name date occurrence/reference. col_value Column name value. col_value_predicted Column name Predicted Value. group_cols Optional character vector column names grouping. color1 Color observed data. color2 Color predicted data.

title: Plot Nowcasting Predictions — plot_nowcast
text: ggplot object comparing observed predicted values.

title: Plot Nowcasting Predictions — plot_nowcast
code: df_nowcast <- data.frame(   date_occurrence = as.Date("2023-01-01") + 0:9,   value_observed = c(10, 12, 15, 13, 18, 20, 22, 24, 25, 20),   value_predicted = c(10, 12, 15, 13, 18, 20, 22, 25, 28, 30) ) plot_nowcast(   df = df_nowcast,   col_value = value_observed,   col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,   col_value_predicted = value_predicted )

title: Plot Nowcast Evaluation Results — plot_nowcast_eval
text: Plots horizontal bar chart nowcasting evaluation metrics per group, selected delay. Two panels shown side side: Differential SMAPE: median per-prediction SMAPE difference (obs minus pred; positive = prediction better), IQR error bar. winrate: share past periods prediction beat raw observed, centered 0 (0.5 = improvement), Wilson 95% CI. Bars coloured whether improvement significant (IQR / CI fully zero) .

title: Plot Nowcast Evaluation Results — plot_nowcast_eval
code: plot_nowcast_eval(   x,   delay = NULL,   color_good = "dodgerblue1",   color_bad = "firebrick1",   alpha_less = 0.35,   ... )

title: Plot Nowcast Evaluation Results — plot_nowcast_eval
text: x nowcast_eval_results S7 object nowcast_eval(). delay Numeric. delay plot. Defaults minimum delay data missing. color_good Character. Colour significantly better predictions.`. color_bad Character. Colour significantly worse predictions.`. alpha_less alpha value "less significant" bars, 0-1. ... Ignored.

title: Plot Nowcast Evaluation Results — plot_nowcast_eval
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot Nowcast Evaluation Results — plot_nowcast_eval
code: input <- generate_test_data() eval_res <- nowcast_eval(   df = input,   col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,   col_date_reporting = date_report,   col_value = value,   n_past = 10,   time_units = "days" ) #> Warning: n_past (10) exceeds available reporting periods (8). Will be using the max available instead: 7 plot(eval_res)  plot(eval_res, delay = 2)

what: Plot Nowcast Evaluation by Delay — plot_nowcast_eval_by_delay
title: Plot Nowcast Evaluation by Delay — plot_nowcast_eval_by_delay
text: Plots evaluation metric function delay, faceted group. y-axis shows much nowcast improves raw observed values, across delays. Background shading indicates direction improvement.

title: Plot Nowcast Evaluation by Delay — plot_nowcast_eval_by_delay
code: plot_nowcast_eval_by_delay(   x,   indicator = "smape_diff_med",   color_good = "dodgerblue1",   color_bad = "firebrick1",   ... )

title: Plot Nowcast Evaluation by Delay — plot_nowcast_eval_by_delay
text: x nowcast_eval_results S7 object nowcast_eval(). indicator Character. metric plot y-axis. One : "smape_diff_med", "winrate". color_good Character. Fill colour "better" region.`. color_bad Character. Fill colour "worse" region.`. ... Ignored.

title: Plot Nowcast Evaluation by Delay — plot_nowcast_eval_by_delay
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot Nowcast Evaluation by Delay — plot_nowcast_eval_by_delay
code: input <- generate_test_data() eval_res <- nowcast_eval(   df = input,   col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,   col_date_reporting = date_report,   col_value = value,   n_past = 10,   time_units = "days" ) #> Warning: n_past (10) exceeds available reporting periods (8). Will be using the max available instead: 7 plot_nowcast_eval_by_delay(eval_res)  plot_nowcast_eval_by_delay(eval_res, indicator = "winrate")

text: x nowcast_eval_results S7 object nowcast_eval(). delay Numeric. delay plot. Defaults minimum delay data missing. color_good Character. Colour prediction beats raw observed.`. color_bad Character. Colour raw observed beats prediction.`. ... Ignored.

title: Plot Nowcast Evaluation Detail Over Time — plot_nowcast_eval_detail
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot Nowcast Evaluation Detail Over Time — plot_nowcast_eval_detail
code: input <- generate_test_data() eval_res <- nowcast_eval(   df = input,   col_date_occurrence = date_occurrence,   col_date_reporting = date_report,   col_value = value,   n_past = 10,   time_units = "days" ) #> Warning: n_past (10) exceeds available reporting periods (8). Will be using the max available instead: 7 plot_nowcast_eval_detail(eval_res)  plot_nowcast_eval_detail(eval_res, delay = 7)

Nowcastr ggplot2 Theme — theme_nowcastr
text: clean, ggplot2 theme. Derived ggplot2::theme_minimal(), bold elements softer colors, bigger texts default.

title: Nowcastr ggplot2 Theme — theme_nowcastr
code: theme_nowcastr(   base_size = 12,   base_family = "sans",   color_bg = "#fbfbfb",   color_grid = "#EEEEEE",   color_title1 = "#444444",   color_title2 = "#555555",   color_title3 = "#666666",   color_title4 = "#777777" )

title: Nowcastr ggplot2 Theme — theme_nowcastr
text: base_size Base font size, pts. base_family Base font family. color_bg Colour plot.background. color_grid Colour major grid lines. color_title1 Darkest text colour (titles, axis texts). color_title2 Secondary text colour (legend text, strips, axis titles). color_title3 Tertiary text colour (legend titles). color_title4 Lightest text colour (subtitles).

title: Nowcastr ggplot2 Theme — theme_nowcastr
text: theme object applied current ggplot.

title: Nowcastr ggplot2 Theme — theme_nowcastr
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(mtcars, aes(mpg, wt)) +   geom_point() +   theme_nowcastr() +   labs(     title = "Fuel efficiency vs. weight",     subtitle = "Source: Motor Trend US magazine",     caption = "Data: mtcars"   )
